title: Sustainability through beauty
excerpt: Sustainability is usually negotiated in spreadsheets and emissions audits.

title: Calm technologies
excerpt: Most contemporary technology is designed to attract attention.

title: Education for the whole person
excerpt: Most of what is called education today is, in practice, training for a specific function.

title: Active, transversal citizenship
excerpt: Citizenship, in the standard account, is something you exercise on a few specific occasions: when you vote, when you attend a town hall, when you write to your representative.

title: Explicitly pro-European
excerpt: We declare this commitment openly because the moment requires it.

title: Critical thinking
excerpt: Critical thinking has become a slogan, which is a problem, because slogans are exactly the kind of language that critical thinking is supposed to push back against.

title: Curiosity and experiment
excerpt: A curious organisation is one that protects the right of its members to follow a question without knowing the answer in advance.

title: Critical technology
excerpt: Critical technology is the slower cousin of digital transformation.

title: Living heritage
excerpt: The default treatment of cultural heritage in much of Europe is curatorial.

title: Responsibility toward the future
excerpt: We are the ancestors of the people who will inherit our decisions.

title: Against disorientation
excerpt: The contemporary information environment is, by structure rather than by accident, hostile to slow thought.
